turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <style>
      .orbit{fill:none;stroke:#1f77b4;stroke-width:3;opacity:0.9}
      .nucleus{fill:#ff6b6b;stroke:#a50000;stroke-width:2}
      .electron{fill:#4ecdc4;stroke:#1b998b;stroke-width:2}
      .shadow{fill:#000;opacity:0.06}
    </style>
  </defs>

  <!-- subtle background -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="220" class="shadow"/>

  <!-- Orbits (ellipses) -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="150" ry="55" class="orbit"/>
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="105" ry="85" class="orbit"/>
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="256" rx="80" ry="120" class="orbit"/>

  <!-- Nucleus -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="38" class="nucleus"/>
  <circle cx="242" cy="246" r="10" fill="#ffd3d3" opacity="0.85"/>

  <!-- Electrons -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="256" r="3" fill="none"/>
  <!-- on first orbit -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="201" r="8" class="electron"/>
  <circle cx="256" cy="311" r="8" class="electron"/>
  <!-- on second orbit -->
  <circle cx="350" cy="268" r="7" class="electron"/>
  <circle cx="168" cy="244" r="7" class="electron"/>
  <!-- on third orbit -->
  <circle cx="316" cy="144" r="6.5" class="electron"/>
  <circle cx="195" cy="356" r="6.5" class="electron"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating an atom drawing**

I need to create a drawing of an "atom" that doesn't include letters. I’m thinking about a simple representation, with the nucleus shown as a circle in the center and two or three orbits around it to represent the electrons. I’ll aim to provide this in an SVG format with a size of 512. Just keep the output as JSON, with no text included. Sounds straightforward enough!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Generating atomic model terms**

I'm trying to identify terms related to a drawing that resembles an atomic model, with a red nucleus and blue electron orbits. I need to provide 20 one-word guesses. I start with: atom, nucleus, electron, orbit, and planetary. I wonder about terms like electron cloud, isotope, proton, neutron, and quark. I need to organize these by confidence, where my top choices are atom and atomic. Eventually, I'll gather 20 unique terms.
